items.title: Pensioner living in car has 'lost faith' in system as he waits for house

children.content: Despite upcoming changes from the WA government to overhaul the housing waitlist, a 68-year-old living in his car says he has lost faith in the system as an advocate calls for faster action.

gallery.altText: Mark Unsworth in car looks to camera.
Mark Unsworth looks out to the skyline above the ocean.
Mark Unsworth in car looks away from the camera from his car seat.

items.title: Dockers down Cats as Bolton takes mark of the year contender

children.content: Fremantle comes from behind to beat Geelong for a 13th straight game, as Shai Bolton hits the heights at Perth Stadium with a Mark of the Year contender.

image.altText: A Fremantle AFL forward flies high and grabs the ball in mid-air, standing on a teammate and a Geelong player.

items.title: Battle-worn Freo fans dare to dream a maiden premiership is within reach

children.content: Hard-bitten Dockers supporters won't want to say it out loud, but as Fremantle flexes more muscle with each passing week, hope is slowly turning to expectation that a first AFL flag could be on the cards.

image.altText: Dockers sing their club song after winning the AFL Round 9 match between the Fremantle Dockers and the Hawthorn Hawks

items.title: Hantavirus ship passenger recounts 'huge shock' as quarantine nears end

image.altText: A passenger on board the cruise ship MV Hondius

children.content: The first Australian to speak publicly about being onboard the MV Hondius shares his experience of the deadly outbreak on the ship, as he prepares to leave a Perth quarantine facility and return home to his family.

items.title: Love of film makes for a perfect mix for this WA farmer

image.altText: a female standing in front of a tractor in the Great Southern of WA.

children.content: Farmer Brianna Jones has worked on some of Western Australia's biggest film productions, such as Blueback, Sweet As, and The Surfer.

items.title: Authorities tape up 50 Perth stores in biggest illegal tobacco blitz under new laws

children.content: Authorities say a recent two-week compliance blitz results in them seizing $11 million of illegal product like cigarettes and vapes, but how much is it disrupting the lucrative industry?

gallery.altText: Shop with glass doors covered in large Store Closed signs

thumbnail.altText: Shop with glass doors covered in large Store Closed signs

items.title: The whipping tree went from a place of pain to one of healing

children.content: In Western Australia's far north there is a "whipping tree" which for decades stood as a reminder of painful times, but has now become a place of healing and reconciliation.

image.altText: group of Indigenous people gathering around a table holding tree stumps

items.title: Interpreters shut out by police despite bungled murder investigation, peak body claims

children.content: More than a decade after Gene Gibson was wrongfully convicted for the murder of Broome tradesman Josh Warneke, Aboriginal interpreters say they are still overlooked by WA Police.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat stands in front of a sign for an Aboriginal interpreting service.

items.title: The 2 minutes and 46 seconds of Hanson's speech that she thinks matters

children.content: When Pauline Hanson stood up at the National Press Club, it was not just for the audience watching on broadcast TV. In the age of social media, the bite-sized clips that come later are far more important.

articles.title: An orange bulldozer is ploughing through the political arena

children.content: Four years ago, Australians decided they didn't want a bulldozer for a prime minister. But now if Gina Rinehart has her way, a big orange one will be ploughing her way through the political arena.

image.altText: pauline hanson gina rinehart bulldozer

articles.title: Pauline Hanson's criticism of Guardian reporter labelled an 'assault on free press'

children.content: Pauline Hanson's public excoriation of a Guardian journalist questioning her at the National Press Club yesterday is an "assault on the freedom of the press", the media union says, as Labor and Liberal MPs criticise the One Nation leader for getting personal with journalists.

image.altText: A woman talks into a microphone

articles.title: Hanson's fiery address creates risk for One Nation

children.content: In one press club appearance, we've learned more about where Hanson stands. And that creates risk for One Nation.

image.altText: A woman with red hair speaks at a lectern.

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: 'Moment of grief': Pakistani police apologise for 9yo Australian girl's death

children.content: Pakistani officials apologise for the fatal shooting of a nine-year-old Australian girl, and outline the violation of protocols that led to her death as they pursue murder charges against the officer involved.

image.altText: Selfie of a man and his daughter on a plane.

items.title: 'Took my breath away': Afghanistan gallery opening soon at Australian War Memorial

children.content: Australian War Memorial director Matt Anderson is asked about an invitation issued to Ben Roberts-Smith, who has been accused of war crimes, to the upcoming opening of a new gallery space dedicated to Australia's service in Afghanistan.

image.altText: A painting of a man lying down, with strong colours and brushstrokes.

items.title: From refugee to Socceroos star: The grassroots club that gave Nestory Irankunda a go

children.content: Long before Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal buoyed Australian fans he made his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves Soccer Club, where he is a hero to many other young players who share a similar background.

articles.title: England's high-octane start betrays World Cup nerves

children.content: England's World Cup opener against Croatia, with a missed penalty and a flaky defence, seemed to be briefly polluted by fear.

image.altText: Harry Kane claps his hands

articles.title: World Cup quick hits: Star whacks US pundit and Mabil's heartwarming moment

children.content: Zlatan Ibrahimovic drops "ignorant" bomb to Fox Sports colleagues, Scotland's fans almost drink Boston dry and Awer Mabil gets starstruck in front of SBS legend David Basheer. Here are the quick hits from today at the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: A collation of a picture of three men.

items.title: 'I'm blaming JD': Key lines from Trump's Iran deal press conference

children.content: Donald Trump has held a press conference as the United States and Tehran have signed a memorandum of understanding to end the war in Iran.

image.altText: Trump with a defeated look on his face while making a fist

items.title: After 27 years, Virginia Trioli takes creative leap and leaves the ABC

children.content: After 27 years as a journalist, broadcaster and program maker at the ABC, Virginia Trioli announces she is leaving to pursue new creative opportunities.

image.altText: Virginia Trioli, wearing a fitted blazer, poses for a photo in a studio next to a spotlight and microphone

items.title: Wife and carer 'dumbfounded' by husband's aged care assessment

children.content: Melbourne man Graham Crossan relies on his wife Gaynor to help him eat, move and even breathe. But a new assessment tool means he has been refused funding for more support.

image.altText: A woman with light blonde hair in a pink shirt rests her hand on the head of a bald man in a chair with an oxygen mask on.

items.title: Uni students tackle 'character-building' remote living through placements

children.content: University student Tarhnee says her survival kit for a nursing prac placement was food, a swag, a chair and a little table for her laptop.

image.altText: A small tent and camping chair.

items.title: Gina Rinehart's iron ore company flags job losses, reportedly in the hundreds

children.content: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Iron Ore has cut jobs a year after merging its two iron ore businesses into a single entity, but won't say how many.

gallery.altText: Gina Rinehart stands at a podium.
Hancock Iron Ore's headquarters in West Perth, viewed from across the road on a clear blue day.

Hancock Iron Ore's headquarters in West Perth, viewed from across the road on a clear blue day.

items.title: 13-year-old's death prompted promises of reform. Her mother says little has changed

image.altText: A young girl poses for a close up photo

children.content: The mother of Kate Savage, whose death at 13 sparked a review of mental health supports for children, says she's lost faith promises of reform will ever be kept.

items.title: Magpies admit Hill is 'a long way away from playing footy'

children.content: Bobby Hill returns to Collingwood training, but coach Craig McRae says it will be some time before the 2023 Norm Smith medallist is ready to play in the AFL again.

image.altText: Bobby Hill at a Collingwood AFL training session in 2026.

items.title: Drone footage shows extent of seal deaths on Australian island

children.content: Research has revealed the devastating impact of bird flu on a remote Australian territory where more than 13,000 baby seals are now believed to have been killed by the virus.

image.altText: NOLh_DroneSeals_1806_0

items.title: Hegseth blasts NATO members, announces review of US forces in Europe

children.content: He said that too many NATO allies failed to support the US on Iran in what he called a "shameful" denial of access, basing and overflight.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth speaks during a press statement on arrival for a meeting of NATO defense ministers

items.title: Ancient oak tree linked to the legend of Robin Hood is dead

children.content: The tree is said to have sheltered Robin Hood, the legendary 13th-century bandit who stole from the rich and gave to the poor and took refuge in Nottingham Forest.

image.altText: An upwards view of tree trunks.

items.title: WA rail union pushes back on proposal for safety lights on trains

children.content: A draft bill mandating more lights on trains is "fundamentally flawed" and would not fix the behaviour of motorists at level crossings, the union for train drivers says.
